mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings across various sectors, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting corporate profits and equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0% over a 10-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows significant annualized growth while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an overly accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures. This suggests future aggressive tightening by the central bank, which will likely compress equity valuations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, indicating no market headwinds from this condition.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a robust and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. In a mid-cycle expansion, this growth provides a stable foundation for positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, as companies benefit from increased economic activity.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative (average return < -0.1%) during periods identified as mid-cycle expansion when GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market. This directly drives consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 performance over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation (p-value > 0.1 for a t-test on correlation coefficient) with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level indicates a weakening housing market, which is a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown and reduced consumer confidence. This typically leads to decreased corporate investment and consumer spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive (average return > 0.1%) in more than 35% of instances where HOUST drops below 400.0 (thousands of units) over a 15-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects the health of the manufacturing and mining sectors. Sustained positive growth indicates strong underlying economic activity and corporate profitability, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment environment, a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a hawkish monetary stance despite labor market weakness. This combination can severely dampen economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as borrowing costs increase and demand slows.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive (average return > 0.1%) in more than 30% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25pp in 3 months while UNRATE remains above 7.0%, over a 15-year backtest.
